items.title: Anime Overload Wiki Hub
items.subtitle: Understand the game loop and start with the right pages
items.intro: Anime Overload is a Roblox anime tower defense experience where players summon Heroes, manage wave defense pressure, and scale into raids, traits, quests, crafting, and dungeon systems.

cards.title: What Is Anime Overload
cards.description: The game combines hero summoning, wave defense maps, in-match upgrades, and long-term growth systems that reward strong roster planning.

cards.title: Core Systems
cards.description: Progression starts with summoning Heroes, leveling key units, rerolling traits, and using duplicate copies for Prestige or Ascension paths.

cards.title: Main Modes
cards.description: Standard stages, raids, quests, and Monarch Dungeon-style content create a clean ladder from easy farming to high-pressure boss mechanics.

cards.title: Quick Links Players Usually Need
cards.description: Most players start with Codes, Heroes, Traits, Story, Quests, Raids, Collections, and Beginner Guide pages to speed up team quality.

items.title: Anime Overload Wiki Codes
items.subtitle: Anime Overload Wiki codes and redeem flow for fast early growth
items.intro: Codes are the fastest no-grind boost for Crystal Shards, Gold, Trait Rerolls, and stars that accelerate your first roster upgrades.

codes.status: Active reward pack for early trait setup
codes.notes: Use immediately to stabilize your first main unit.
Ideal before a summon session or reroll burst.

steps: Launch Anime Overload on Roblox and open the News menu.
Paste one active code exactly as listed.
Press Redeem Code and confirm your inventory update.
Spend rerolls and currency on your strongest early wave clearer first.

cards.title: Why These Rewards Matter
cards.description: Crystal Shards support summon and progression systems, Gold covers upgrades, and reroll resources increase your odds of strong trait outcomes.

cards.title: Where New Codes Usually Drop
cards.description: Official Discord announcements are the fastest source. Patch-day guide coverage and update notes usually mirror new drops quickly.

items.title: Anime Overload Wiki Tier List
items.subtitle: Build around top carries, then lock support and economy consistency
items.intro: The launch meta favors premium carries with reliable AoE wave clear and one support or economy slot to smooth difficult stages.

columns.title: Top Carries Right Now
items: Strongest Alien (Burst) for late-wave pressure.
Susono for stable high-end damage output.
Ener (Lightning) for clear speed and boss support.
Sokono as a high-value endgame damage anchor.

columns.title: Best Value Picks for New Players
items: Killua remains a strong Epic-level carry in early routes.
Bald Hero variants are solid bridge units for progression.
Kid Psychic can hold utility slots until rarer pulls arrive.
Keep one slot flexible to upgrade into Secret units quickly.

cards.title: Support and Economy Picks
cards.description: Law (Death Surgeon) contributes team utility, Escanor supports economy pacing, and Inoomaki adds control effects for safer clears.

cards.title: Recommended Team Template
cards.description: Start with one main carry, one consistent AoE clearer, one economy or support unit, then replace temporary fillers as top-end heroes enter your box.

items.title: Anime Overload Wiki Beginner Guide
items.subtitle: A practical first-hour plan for clean and fast progression
items.intro: New players should secure code rewards first, prioritize AoE units, and learn raid mechanics early to avoid losing tempo in midgame content.
steps: Redeem all active codes before your first major summon spend.
Build around your best early AoE Hero for wave coverage.
Push standard wave stages to farm Gold, Crystals, and upgrade mats.
Learn crouch timing for sleep or stun boss mechanics.
Transition into raids, traits, and dungeon material farming once your core trio is stable.

cards.title: Your First Hour
cards.description: Use all free rewards, lock one damage core, and avoid over-investing in weak temporary units.

cards.title: Best Early Priorities
cards.description: Invest upgrades into one reliable unit at a time, save currency for strong banners, and avoid scattered spending.

cards.title: Mechanics You Need Early
cards.description: Bosses can punish poor movement with sleep or stun. Dungeon hazards can remove placed units if ignored for too long.

cards.title: When Progression Opens Up
cards.description: Once basics are stable, move into raids, dungeon routes, traits, and evolution material loops for endgame scaling.

items.title: Anime Overload Wiki Traits Guide
items.subtitle: Trait effects, reroll priorities, and role-based matching
items.intro: Traits define damage ceilings and utility efficiency. A focused reroll plan saves resources and upgrades the right units faster.

columns.title: High Priority Trait Targets
items: Main carry: maximize burst or sustained DPS traits.
Wave clear unit: prioritize AoE consistency.
Support slot: value control and team-impact traits.
Economy slot: secure stable farming value first.

columns.title: Reroll Management Rules
items: Reroll only on units that survive your next progression tier.
Spend bulk rerolls after code refreshes and events.
Keep practical high-roll traits instead of chasing perfect rolls every time.
Reserve tickets for carries with long-term relevance.

cards.title: Trait Value by Role
cards.description: DPS units prioritize raw damage scaling, while support units gain more from utility uptime and control effects.

cards.title: When to Stop Rerolling
cards.description: Stop once a unit reaches a strong practical roll that clears your current target content. Move resources to the next bottleneck.

items.title: Anime Overload Wiki Heroes
items.subtitle: Core unit roles and roster structure for stable clears
items.intro: Heroes should be evaluated by role fit, map coverage, and upgrade efficiency rather than rarity alone.

cards.title: Strongest Alien (Burst)
cards.description: High-end damage carry for difficult waves and boss pressure.

cards.title: Susono
cards.description: Reliable endgame damage profile with strong map value.

cards.title: Ener (Lightning)
cards.description: Excellent wave pressure and useful burst windows.

cards.title: Law (Death Surgeon)
cards.description: Support utility that improves team stability in high-pressure content.

cards.title: Escanor
cards.description: Economy-friendly option for smoother long farming sessions.

cards.title: Killua
cards.description: Strong early value unit that keeps progression efficient before premium pulls.

items.title: Anime Overload Wiki Progression Guide
items.subtitle: Move from launch setup to endgame loops with clear milestones
items.intro: A stable progression route prevents wasted currency and keeps upgrades aligned with content difficulty.

timeline.title: Launch Setup
timeline.detail: Redeem codes, secure one AoE core, and push early stages for baseline currency.

timeline.title: Roster Stabilization
timeline.detail: Build a carry-AoE-support trio and stop over-investing in temporary units.

timeline.title: Trait Optimization
timeline.detail: Use rerolls on long-term units and lock practical high-value traits.

timeline.title: Raid Transition
timeline.detail: Start targeted raid clears for reward loops and endgame materials.

timeline.title: Dungeon + Evolution
timeline.detail: Farm Pure Stars and evolve key units to secure late-game scaling.

cards.title: Resource Priority
cards.description: Spend on roster quality first, then trait upgrades, then broad collection targets.

cards.title: Common Progression Leak
cards.description: Frequent rerolls on low-value units and too many unfinished upgrades slow progression dramatically.

items.title: Anime Overload Wiki Story
items.subtitle: World progression, stage routes, and practical drop focus
items.intro: Story maps are the backbone of progression and your first reliable route for core upgrade resources.
steps: Clear early worlds with one optimized AoE core.
Repeat high-efficiency stages for your current bottleneck resource.
Upgrade only the units that improve your next map clear threshold.
Rotate into raids once story clears are stable and fast.

cards.title: World Overview
cards.description: Each world introduces heavier wave pressure and rewards stronger placement discipline.

cards.title: Act Goals
cards.description: Prioritize consistent clears over risky speed routes when your roster is still forming.

cards.title: Drop Routing
cards.description: Track which stages feed your next evolve requirement and keep a tight farming rotation.

items.title: Anime Overload Wiki Raids
items.subtitle: Raid entry flow, boss mechanics, and high-value reward routing
items.intro: Raids push your roster and mechanics harder than story mode and are central to mid-to-endgame growth.

columns.title: Raid Team Roles
items: Main boss killer for burst windows.
Reliable AoE for wave pressure control.
Support/control slot for stability.
Optional economy slot when map pace allows it.

columns.title: Boss Survival Rules
items: Respect stun and sleep patterns with movement timing.
Place key units where they survive hazard paths.
Track cooldown windows before committing major upgrades.
Rebuild quickly after boss phase shifts.

cards.title: Reward Focus
cards.description: Raids are a major source of progression materials and should be chained with your evolve roadmap.

cards.title: Best Entry Timing
cards.description: Enter raids after your core trio is upgraded enough to avoid resource-draining wipe loops.

items.title: Anime Overload Wiki Summon & Reroll Guide
items.subtitle: Banner timing, reroll budgeting, and smart spend decisions
items.intro: Summon and reroll choices decide whether your progression accelerates or stalls. Discipline here has the highest long-term value.
steps: Use fresh code rewards before opening a new banner cycle.
Set a target carry and stop once that role is secured.
Save reroll tickets for units you will keep across multiple progression tiers.
Avoid rerolling temporary fillers unless they block your immediate clear route.
Re-evaluate spending after each update drop and event rotation.

cards.title: When to Summon
cards.description: Summon when your roster lacks a clear carry or AoE anchor, not just when currency is available.

cards.title: When to Save
cards.description: If your current lineup clears target content, hold currency for stronger banners or event windows.

items.title: Anime Overload Wiki Secret Units
items.subtitle: High-value chase units, unlock pressure, and farming routes
items.intro: Secret units are long-term power spikes and should be pursued with planned routes instead of random farming.

columns.title: Secret Unit Farming Focus
items: Farm content that directly feeds your secret target requirements.
Use stable auto-capable routes for repeated clears.
Pair secret farming with material goals to avoid split sessions.
Track drop progress across update cycles.

columns.title: Roster Integration
items: Replace temporary carries first when a secret unit is secured.
Transfer high-value traits to the new long-term anchor.
Rebuild team around new range and damage profile.
Adjust raid and story routes after each major upgrade.

cards.title: Susono Secret Track
cards.description: A common high-interest secret path for players aiming at endgame clears.

cards.title: Banner Secret Rotation
cards.description: Monitor update windows for better chase timing and lower opportunity cost.

items.title: Anime Overload Wiki Collections
items.subtitle: Collection categories, relic progress, and completion strategy
items.intro: Collections add long-tail progression value and provide a clean structure for tracking long sessions.
steps: Group targets by the content route you already farm.
Prioritize collection pieces tied to your current progression bottleneck.
Track missing relics and rotate maps based on highest completion gain.
Review collection status after every major patch cycle.

cards.title: Collection Categories
cards.description: Break collections into raid-linked, story-linked, and event-linked sets for better farming control.

cards.title: Relic Progress Tracking
cards.description: Use a simple checklist format to avoid duplicate runs and keep progress visible.

cards.title: Completion Value
cards.description: Collection routing helps convert general grinding into measurable milestones.

items.title: Anime Overload Wiki Evolution Materials
items.subtitle: Map your Pure Stars and shard spending to your best units
items.intro: Material efficiency drives endgame growth. Route every farm session toward your next evolve requirement.

columns.title: Core Material Targets
items: Crystal Shards for progression and summon-related systems.
Pure Stars for deeper hero evolution paths.
Mythic evolve items for top-end upgrades.
Raid-linked drops for bottleneck unlocks.

columns.title: Unit-to-Material Mapping
items: Prioritize carries with immediate clear impact.
Assign each farming session to one evolve milestone.
Track material deficits before spending rerolls.
Only branch into side units after core upgrades finish.

cards.title: Material Matrix Setup
cards.description: Maintain a simple matrix of unit, required materials, current stock, and next route.

cards.title: Upgrade Order
cards.description: Upgrade your most-used carry first, then wave-clear support, then situational options.

items.title: Anime Overload Wiki Quest Guide
items.subtitle: Quest priorities that fit daily play and weekly progression
items.intro: Quests are a stable progression layer and should be routed alongside your story and raid goals.

cards.title: Quest Overview
cards.description: Use quests as a daily baseline for guaranteed progress and resource stability.

cards.title: Quest Types
cards.description: Rotate between fast-complete objectives and high-value milestones based on session length.

cards.title: Best Quests to Prioritize
cards.description: Prioritize tasks that overlap with your current story push, raid prep, or material farming target.

cards.title: Quest Reward Routing
cards.description: Convert rewards directly into the next needed upgrade to keep momentum high.

steps: Start with short daily quests to secure baseline resources.
Chain longer quests with story or raid sessions.
Spend rewards on your current roster bottleneck.
Reset priorities after each update event.

items.title: Anime Overload Wiki Mounts
items.subtitle: Mount unlock routes, collection value, and showcase planning
items.intro: Mounts improve account depth and collection identity while adding another consistent long-tail target.

cards.title: All Mounts Checklist
cards.description: Track unlocked mounts by source and keep one route per session to avoid scattered progress.

cards.title: How to Unlock Faster
cards.description: Combine mount progress with your existing story, raid, or event objectives.

cards.title: Cosmetic vs Practical Value
cards.description: Prioritize unlocks that align with your broader progression plan, then complete showcase targets.

items.title: Anime Overload Wiki Update Log
items.subtitle: Latest update headlines, events, and patch-driven action items
items.intro: Use update notes as a planning layer for summons, rerolls, and farming route changes.

timeline.title: Early Access Opened
timeline.detail: Public launch brought core progression loops, code rewards, and first-wave meta formation.

timeline.title: Code Refresh Waves
timeline.detail: Major code drops expanded reroll and currency options for faster roster upgrades.

timeline.title: Carry Priorities Shifted
timeline.detail: Tier choices moved toward premium carries with reliable AoE and boss consistency.

timeline.title: More Raid-Centric Routing
timeline.detail: Players increasingly route progress through raids to accelerate endgame materials.

timeline.title: Discord Announcements
timeline.detail: Official Discord remains the fastest source for code drops and event timing.

cards.title: How to Use This Log
cards.description: After each patch, review carry ranking changes, reroll value shifts, and route updates before spending.

cards.title: Weekly Review Habit
cards.description: A weekly check of updates and code channels keeps your progression aligned with current opportunities.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything players ask about Anime Overload Wiki progression and systems

questions.question: How does Anime Overload Wiki help new players start faster?
questions.answer: Anime Overload Wiki organizes codes, beginner routes, and team-building priorities so new players can clear early stages with less trial and error.

questions.question: Where does Anime Overload Wiki track the latest codes and redeem flow?
questions.answer: Anime Overload Wiki keeps active code lists, redeem steps, and practical reward priorities in the codes module for quick execution.

questions.question: What does Anime Overload Wiki include for tier list planning?
questions.answer: Anime Overload Wiki covers top carries, value picks, support options, and when to replace temporary units as your roster improves.

questions.question: Can Anime Overload Wiki guide trait rerolls and summon spending?
questions.answer: Anime Overload Wiki provides role-based trait priorities and reroll budgeting rules to keep resources focused on long-term units.

questions.question: Does Anime Overload Wiki explain raids, quests, and story routing together?
questions.answer: Anime Overload Wiki links raid roles, quest priorities, and story farming routes into one progression path so each session has a clear objective.

questions.question: How often should players review Anime Overload Wiki update log entries?
questions.answer: Anime Overload Wiki update notes are most useful when checked weekly and after each major patch or code drop.
